human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for gaming-floor specificity, jackpot and fill workflow fidelity, player-account and surveillance evidence quality, cash and compliance boundary discipline, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to gaming-floor execution, jackpot, fill, credit, player-account, surveillance-sensitive, and patron-protection workflows rather than cage authority, AML disposition, tax advice, device repair, or regulatory approval.
Authority sources cover commercial and tribal gaming controls, official surveillance and checklist guidance, FinCEN casino compliance posture, IRS jackpot documentation, and self-exclusion handling.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal floor execution, missing artifacts, unsupported completion, suspicious cash behavior, patron-protection escalation, jurisdictional conflict, tenant adaptation, and cross-system closure disagreement.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/casinos-gaming-and-wagering/gaming-floor-specialist/ so gaming-floor work is distinct from adjacent cage, surveillance, AML, device-tech, compliance, and accounting lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported payouts, self-exclusion, suspicious cash activity, device authority, and source-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated gaming floor specialist for evidence-backed floor execution, patron-service issue handling, and regulated exception routing.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
